runs.final_dockerfile:
FROM ubuntu:jammy-20260217

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install build and runtime dependencies for OpenPose CPU-only build
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  build-essential cmake git pkg-config wget ca-certificates python3-dev python3-pip \
  libprotobuf-dev protobuf-compiler libopencv-dev libboost-all-dev \
  libgflags-dev libgoogle-glog-dev libeigen3-dev libopenblas-dev libatlas-base-dev \
  libhdf5-dev libjpeg-dev libtiff5-dev zlib1g-dev libgl1-mesa-dev \
  libx11-dev libglew-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# OpenPose is built from source in /workspace
WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the entire repository into the image
COPY . /workspace

# Configure and build OpenPose in CPU-only mode
RUN cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DGPU_MODE=CPU_ONLY -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON -DWITH_OPENCV_WITH_OPENGL=OFF -DBUILD_PYTHON=OFF \
  -DDL_FRAMEWORK=PYTORCH \
  -DDOWNLOAD_BODY_25_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_BODY_COCO_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_BODY_MPI_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_FACE_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_HAND_MODEL=OFF && \
  cmake --build build -j$(nproc)

# Default to an interactive shell to inspect the image
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

- Missing packages/files mentioned
  - Missing OpenPose OpenPose/OpenPose caffe integration: “No download info given for 'openpose_lib'”
  - Source directory /workspace/3rdparty/caffe is not an existing non-empty directory (i.e., the expected caffe subdir is missing or in an invalid state)

- Version mismatch / toolchain notes
  - Build output shows C compiler is GNU 11.4.0 (GCC 11)
  - Earlier apt output indicates attempts to upgrade to gcc-12-base and related libgcc/libstdc++6, implying a mixed/redirected toolchain state during package install. This may contribute to version-compatibility issues with the OpenPose build.

- Context

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM ubuntu:jammy-20260217

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install build and runtime dependencies for OpenPose CPU-only build
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  build-essential cmake git pkg-config wget ca-certificates python3-dev python3-pip \
  libprotobuf-dev protobuf-compiler libopencv-dev libboost-all-dev \
  libgflags-dev libgoogle-glog-dev libeigen3-dev libopenblas-dev libatlas-base-dev \
  libhdf5-dev libjpeg-dev libtiff5-dev zlib1g-dev libgl1-mesa-dev \
  libx11-dev libglew-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# OpenPose is built from source in /workspace
WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the entire repository into the image
COPY . /workspace

# Configure and build OpenPose in CPU-only mode
RUN cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DGPU_MODE=CPU_ONLY -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON -DWITH_OPENCV_WITH_OPENGL=OFF -DBUILD_PYTHON=OFF && \
  cmake --build build -j$(nproc)

# Default to an interactive shell to inspect the image
CMD ["/bin/bash"]
"}

{"path": ".dockerignore", "content": "# Ignore git repository and IDE/editor files
.git
.gitignore
*.swp
*.log
*.cache
*.idea
*.vscode
build
CMakeFiles
CMakeCache.txt
-Debug
-Release
*.o
*.a
*.so
*.dylib
*.exe
*.dll

# Ignore Python caches
__pycache__
*.pyc

# Node modules and npm/yarn
node_modules
package-lock.json
yarn.lock
"}

- Ensure ExternalProject_Add for openpose_lib provides SOURCE_DIR with an existing non-empty directory or a valid URL/GIT_REPOSITORY/DOWNLOAD_COMMAND.

- Correct the OpenPose-Caffe integration step to supply proper download/source configuration for openpose_lib.
- If appropriate, add .dockerignore and pin base image digest to improve reproducibility and build speed.

Root cause in logs
- CMake error during ExternalProject setup:
  CMake Error at /usr/share/cmake-3.22/Modules/ExternalProject.cmake:2882 (message):
  No download info given for 'openpose_lib' and its source directory:

- The build attempt notes: “Caffe will be downloaded from source now.” followed by “fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git” (two occurrences), indicating an attempt to fetch/build Caffe from source but the repository state is invalid.
- After this, the log shows: “Download the models.” and finally “Configuring incomplete, errors occurred!” with a pointer to CMakeOutput.log.

Environment and version clues
- Base image: ubuntu:jammy-20260217
- Compiler/toolchain: GNU 11.4.0 (C and C++ compilers)
- Found dependencies: Protobuf 3.12.4; OpenCV 4.5.4
- Build path reference: /workspace/ (CMakeOutput.log at /workspace/build/CMakeFiles/CMakeOutput.log)

Missing files/packages mentioned
- Missing download/source info for openpose_lib; the ExternalProject_Add requires a SOURCE_DIR or one of URL, GIT_REPOSITORY, etc.
- /workspace/3rdparty/caffe is not an existing non-empty directory, causing the ExternalProject to fail.

Notes
- Root cause is the OpenPose/OpenPose-Caffe integration step lacking proper download/source configuration for openpose_lib, coupled with an invalid /workspace/3rdparty/caffe directory.

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM ubuntu:jammy-20260217

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install build and runtime dependencies for OpenPose CPU-only build
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  build-essential cmake git pkg-config wget ca-certificates python3-dev python3-pip \
  libprotobuf-dev protobuf-compiler libopencv-dev libboost-all-dev \
  libgflags-dev libgoogle-glog-dev libeigen3-dev libopenblas-dev libatlas-base-dev \
  libhdf5-dev libjpeg-dev libtiff5-dev zlib1g-dev libgl1-mesa-dev \
  libx11-dev libglew-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# OpenPose is built from source in /workspace
WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the entire repository into the image
COPY . /workspace

# Configure and build OpenPose in CPU-only mode
RUN cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DGPU_MODE=CPU_ONLY -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON -DWITH_OPENCV_WITH_OPENGL=OFF -DBUILD_PYTHON=OFF \
  -DDL_FRAMEWORK=PYTORCH \
  -DDOWNLOAD_BODY_25_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_BODY_COCO_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_BODY_MPI_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_FACE_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_HAND_MODEL=OFF && \
  cmake --build build -j$(nproc)

# Default to an interactive shell to inspect the image
CMD ["/bin/bash"]
"}

artifacts.content:
FROM ubuntu:jammy-20260217

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install build and runtime dependencies for OpenPose CPU-only build
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  build-essential cmake git pkg-config wget ca-certificates python3-dev python3-pip \
  libprotobuf-dev protobuf-compiler libopencv-dev libboost-all-dev \
  libgflags-dev libgoogle-glog-dev libeigen3-dev libopenblas-dev libatlas-base-dev \
  libhdf5-dev libjpeg-dev libtiff5-dev zlib1g-dev libgl1-mesa-dev \
  libx11-dev libglew-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# OpenPose is built from source in /workspace
WORKDIR /workspace

# Copy the entire repository into the image
COPY . /workspace

# Configure and build OpenPose in CPU-only mode
RUN cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DGPU_MODE=CPU_ONLY -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON -DWITH_OPENCV_WITH_OPENGL=OFF -DBUILD_PYTHON=OFF \
  -DDL_FRAMEWORK=PYTORCH \
  -DDOWNLOAD_BODY_25_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_BODY_COCO_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_BODY_MPI_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_FACE_MODEL=OFF -DDOWNLOAD_HAND_MODEL=OFF && \
  cmake --build build -j$(nproc)

# Default to an interactive shell to inspect the image
CMD ["/bin/bash"]
